description: Compile explicit fluid initial conditions, simulate solver-owned particle truth, reconstruct a render surface, replay it with geometry-matched assets in Unreal Engine, and publish synchronized RGB, metric depth, and instance segmentation.
physical_assumptions: Genesis SPH owns particle-state truth. Genesis native Rasterizer or Unreal Engine may render the same surface cache, but each adapter declares its renderer and never re-labels the mesh as solver truth.
Fluid initial conditions are explicit: bounded_volume, container_fill, or emitter. Geometry is only meaningful for bounded_volume and is never a global default for all fluid cases.
Solver steps may be more frequent than output frames, but every output timestamp must map to an exact solver state.
A reconstructed mesh is a coupling representation, not a replacement for particle truth.
A declared velocity field initializes the particles once at frame zero; later particle states must come only from the solver.
A moving container receives only its declared kinematic schedule; the fluid response, transfer path, splash, spill, and settling remain solver-owned.
Every container collider must represent an open interior and match the visible asset geometry; a sealed convex hull is not a valid liquid vessel.
The solver-to-Unreal bridge converts right-handed meters to left-handed centimeters and uses one named pitch-yaw-roll conversion for both initial placement and per-frame replay.
Unreal surface assets are content-addressed by particle-state truth and reconstructed-frame hashes; cached UAssets may be reused only when that fingerprint matches.
verifier_rules: Particle count and stable identities remain constant.
Every output frame has a non-empty closed manifold surface mesh.
Particles remain inside the declared basin within tolerance.
Gravity produces a lower final center of mass than the initial state.
For prefilled-water buoyancy cases, low effective-density bodies remain above the basin floor, dense bodies reach it, and the declared float/sink separation is observed.
A declared splash must raise fluid particles above the initial liquid surface by the configured minimum.
A distinctive fluid material remains observable in RGB before a smoke run can promote to a complete candidate.
Instance segmentation with an instance mapping declares and satisfies palette closure.
Every camera declared dynamic has non-zero runtime motion.
A declared uniform or swirling initial velocity field must meet its speed, direction, displacement, or rise thresholds.
Container-transfer cases verify initial source occupancy, final receiver capture, spill fraction, source evacuation duration, and maximum one-frame source-fraction drop before rendering.
The visible source and receiver assets must carry their content hashes and declare asset_geometry_match=true for the solver collision profiles.
Any non-uniform vessel adaptation records scale_xyz in asset lineage and must preserve the solver interior width, depth, floor, and rim rather than only sharing a coarse shape label.
RGB, metric-depth EXR, instance-segmentation EXR, camera truth, and solver state use the same frame count and timebase for every declared view.
Every run publishes one RGB, depth, and segmentation overall in addition to per-view previews; EXR sequences and particle caches remain canonical truth.
A generated or reconstructed surface remains local_preview until a reference-grade surface method and calibration gate are declared.
repair_suggestions: Check SPH time step and particle size.
Use rigid Plane colliders for the Genesis SPH basin.
Increase liquid volume or reconstruction smoothing when the final fluid layer is under-resolved.
Fix the active surface material/render proxy before expanding an RGB smoke into a multi-camera candidate.
Quantize instance masks to black plus the declared stable instance palette and re-run palette closure.
Drive tracking cameras from the particle center trajectory rather than a static proxy transform.
Replace sealed convex collision with an open axisymmetric profile or another interior collider that matches the selected vessel asset.
Probe the solver landing distribution before moving the receiver; do not conceal a solver miss by shifting only the Unreal asset.
Use the shared runtime_rotator conversion for spawn and replay when the fluid follows a different apparent container tilt in Unreal.
Tune source motion and fluid parameters from occupancy and spill traces, not from the rendered video alone.
Invalidate and re-import the Unreal surface sequence whenever particle truth or any reconstructed mesh hash changes.
